edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SmolLM3's chat template borrows Qwen3's pattern of pre-filling the model's response with empty think blocks in non-reasoning mode to ensure direct answers.

anchor_list.excerpt: In non-reasoning mode, we pre-fill the model's response with empty think blocks, similar to Qwen3, to ensure direct answers without explicit reasoning.
anchor_list.explanation: Cites Qwen3's empty-think-block pattern as the inspiration for SmolLM3's non-reasoning-mode chat template.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: SmolLM3-3B (chat / dual instruct+reasoning) is initialized from SmolLM3-3B-Base after long-context extension and then post-trained via reasoning mid-training, SFT, APO alignment, and merging.

anchor_list.excerpt: Base model: https://hf.co/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-Base Instruct and reasoning model: https://hf.co/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
anchor_list.explanation: Both releases share the same SmolLM3-3B family; the chat release continues training from the Base checkpoint via the documented post-training pipeline.

anchor_list.excerpt: base_model: - HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-Base ... Post-training included midtraining on 140B reasoning tokens followed by supervised fine-tuning and alignment via Anchored Preference Optimization (APO).
anchor_list.explanation: Frontmatter declares the weight inheritance and the summary describes the post-training continuation from Base to chat.
anchor_list.position: YAML frontmatter base_model field; Model Summary paragraph

anchor_list.excerpt:
base_model:
  - HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-Base
anchor_list.explanation: HuggingFace YAML metadata declares SmolLM3-3B-Base as the parent checkpoint for SmolLM3-3B.

anchor_list.excerpt: The pretraining consists of these stages ... After the main pretraining we improved the model in a mid-training stage for long context and reasoning ... The resulting model is the checkpoint we are releasing today.
anchor_list.explanation: Source describes the continuous training cascade from the Base model through mid-training, post-training, and merging into the final released SmolLM3-3B.

anchor_list.excerpt:
model_name_or_path: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-Base
model_revision: main
anchor_list.explanation: Pins the 3B-Base revision as the mid-training start.

description_variants: SmolLM3-3B (chat / dual instruct+reasoning) is initialized from SmolLM3-3B-Base after long-context extension and then post-trained via reasoning mid-training, SFT, APO alignment, and merging.

SmolLM3-3B chat/instruct is initialized from the SmolLM3-3B-Base pretrained checkpoint and continues with midtraining on 140B reasoning tokens, supervised fine-tuning, and APO alignment to produce the released instruct model.

SmolLM3-3B (3B-parameter hybrid-reasoning instruct model) is initialized from the SmolLM3-3B-Base pretrain checkpoint and continued through midtraining on 140B reasoning tokens, supervised fine-tuning, and APO alignment.

SmolLM3-3B (the released instruct/reasoning model) inherits weights from the SmolLM3-3B-Base checkpoint via a cascade of long-context mid-training, reasoning mid-training, SFT, APO alignment, and final model merging.

SmolLM3-3B (final Instruct release) is initialized at the mid-training stage from HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-Base; the mid-trained checkpoint lives at HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B-checkpoints (revision it-mid-training).

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: Reasoning mid-training (35B tokens, 4 epochs ~140B) sources general reasoning data from open-thoughts/OpenThoughts3-1.2M.

anchor_list.excerpt: Our mid-training dataset contained 35B tokens sourced from Open Thought's OpenThoughts3-1.2M
anchor_list.explanation: Names OpenThoughts3-1.2M as a reasoning-mid-training source.
Identifies OpenThoughts3-1.2M as the second component of the smoltalk2 Mid mix with row/token weight.

anchor_list.excerpt: OpenThoughts3-1.2M: 1133524 rows. ... OpenThoughts3-1.2M: 435193 rows where we remove the reasoning trace.
anchor_list.explanation: Cites OpenThoughts3-1.2M as a SFT mix component in both think and no_think forms.
anchor_list.position: ### SFT, think list and no_think list

anchor_list.excerpt: Our mid-training dataset contained 35B tokens sourced from Open Thought's OpenThoughts3-1.2M
anchor_list.explanation: Source explicitly names OpenThoughts3-1.2M as a reasoning-mid-training data source.

description_variants: Reasoning mid-training (35B tokens, 4 epochs ~140B) sources general reasoning data from open-thoughts/OpenThoughts3-1.2M.
Mid-training of SmolLM3-3B consumed OpenThoughts3-1.2M (1.14M rows, 16.46B tokens, 23.75% of Mid examples; 46.81% of Mid tokens) as the second component of the smoltalk2 Mid mix.
SFT consumed OpenThoughts3-1.2M as both a `think` split (1133524 rows, 86.74% of SFT tokens at weight 0.02) and as a `no_think` variant where reasoning traces were stripped (435193 rows).
OpenThoughts3-1.2M used in the reasoning mid-training stage of SmolLM3 (35B tokens total mixture, 4 epochs / ~140B tokens trained); checkpoint feeds into subsequent SFT.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: Reasoning mid-training also sources data from a subset of NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 containing R1-generated reasoning traces.

anchor_list.excerpt: and a subset from NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1

anchor_list.excerpt: Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset: 3.64M rows ... Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset_reasoning_r1 | 1 | 3644790 | 76.25 | 18707.9 | 53.19

anchor_list.excerpt: a subset from NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
anchor_list.explanation: Source names the Llama-Nemotron Post-Training Dataset (v1.1) as the second reasoning-mid-training source.

description_variants: Reasoning mid-training also sources data from a subset of NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 containing R1-generated reasoning traces.

Mid-training of SmolLM3-3B consumed the reasoning_r1 split of Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset (3.64M rows, 18.7B tokens, 76.25% of Mid examples; 53.19% of Mid tokens) as a component of the smoltalk2 Mid mix.

A subset of NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 containing R1 reasoning traces was used in the reasoning mid-training of SmolLM3 (35B tokens total mixture, 4 epochs / ~140B tokens trained).

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: The Llama-Nemotron subset used in mid-training carries reasoning traces generated by DeepSeek-R1; SmolLM3-3B's reasoning data therefore originates from R1 outputs.

anchor_list.excerpt: a subset from NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
anchor_list.explanation: States that the mid-training data includes R1-generated reasoning traces, making DeepSeek-R1 a generator of training content for SmolLM3-3B.

anchor_list.excerpt: a subset from NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 with reasoning traces from R1
anchor_list.explanation: Source attributes the reasoning traces in the dataset to DeepSeek R1, making R1 the upstream generator of mid-training content.

description_variants: The Llama-Nemotron subset used in mid-training carries reasoning traces generated by DeepSeek-R1; SmolLM3-3B's reasoning data therefore originates from R1 outputs.

The Llama-Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1.1 subset used in reasoning mid-training contained reasoning traces generated by DeepSeek-R1 — i.e., R1's reasoning traces are training content for SmolLM3 via that dataset.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: The mid-training mixture is also released as part of HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2 (the 'mid' subset), per author confirmations in the blog comments.

anchor_list.excerpt: We released them in SmolTalk2. The dataset has the Mid, and SFT and Preference subsets.
anchor_list.explanation: Author confirms that the mid-training data is released within HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2.

anchor_list.excerpt: We released all the datasets we used for training in SmolTalk2. The dataset card details which datasets were generated using Qwen3-32B.
anchor_list.explanation: Author confirms SmolTalk2 is the release of all training datasets, and the dataset card identifies Qwen3-32B-generated subsets.

anchor_list.excerpt: We released them in SmolTalk2. The dataset has the Mid, and SFT and Preference subsets.
anchor_list.explanation: Author confirms that the APO preference mixture is released in HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2.

anchor_list.excerpt: three subsets (Mid, SFT, Preference) that correspond to the three phases of Post-Training for SmolLM3-3B ... The mid-training dataset has a total of 4.8M rows
anchor_list.explanation: Designates the Mid config of smoltalk2 as the mid-training data for SmolLM3-3B.

anchor_list.excerpt: The total mix consists of 25 datasets, which we decontaminated to remove samples present in the benchmarks used for evaluation
anchor_list.explanation: Identifies the SFT config of smoltalk2 as SmolLM3-3B's SFT data.

anchor_list.excerpt: We used two datasets to train SmolLM3-3B with APO, which has a total of 447k rows.
anchor_list.explanation: Designates the Preference config of smoltalk2 as APO data for SmolLM3-3B.

anchor_list.excerpt:
id: HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2
      config: Mid
      split: Llama_Nemotron_Post_Training_Dataset_reasoning_r1 ... split: OpenThoughts3_1.2M
anchor_list.explanation: Two splits of smoltalk2 'Mid' config drive mid-training.

anchor_list.excerpt: id: HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2 ... config: SFT ... split: smoltalk_smollm3_everyday_conversations_no_think ... split: tulu_3_sft_personas_instruction_following_no_think ... split: hermes_function_calling_v1_no_think ... split: OpenHermes_2.5_no_think ... split: OpenThoughts3_1.2M_no_think_no_think ... split: smolagents_toolcalling_traces_think ... split: s1k_1.1_think
anchor_list.explanation: YAML enumerates the 25 smoltalk2 SFT splits used.

anchor_list.excerpt:
config: Preference
      split: llama_3.1_tulu_3_8b_preference_mixture_no_think ... split: tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think
anchor_list.explanation: Two preference splits drive APO-zero training.

description_variants: The mid-training mixture is also released as part of HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2 (the 'mid' subset), per author confirmations in the blog comments.
SFT data is part of HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2 (the 'sft' subset), the release of all SmolLM3 post-training mixtures.
APO preference data is also released as part of HuggingFaceTB/smoltalk2 (the 'preference' subset), per the SmolTalk2 release.
SmolLM3-3B's mid-training phase consumed the `Mid` config of smoltalk2 (4.78M rows, ~35.17B tokens) prior to SFT and preference optimization.

SFT phase of SmolLM3-3B consumed the `SFT` config of smoltalk2 (3.38M examples, ~19.29B tokens), an aggregate of 25 underlying datasets (10 think + 15 no_think) decontaminated against eval benchmarks.
Preference (APO) phase of SmolLM3-3B consumed the `Preference` config of smoltalk2 (446886 rows total).
Mid-training trains on the smoltalk2 'Mid' config — the Llama_Nemotron_Post_Training_Dataset_reasoning_r1 split and the OpenThoughts3_1.2M split, each at weight 1.0 — packing on, max_length 32768.

SmolLM3-3B SFT trains on the smoltalk2 'SFT' config across 25 named splits (everyday_convs_no_think, systemchats_30k_no_think, tulu_3_sft_personas_instruction_following_no_think, hermes_function_calling_v1_no_think, smol_magpie_ultra_no_think, multilingual_8languages_lang_5_no_think, table_gpt_no_think, OpenHermes_2.5_no_think, OpenThoughts3_1.2M_no_think_no_think, Mixture_of_Thoughts_science_no_think, explore_instruct_rewriting_no_think, smol_rewrite_no_think, smol_summarize_no_think, LongAlign_64k_context_lang_annotated_lang_6_no_think, multi_turn_reasoning_if_think, everyday_convs_reasoning_Qwen3_32B_think, systemchats_Qwen3_32B_think, xlam_traces_no_think, smolagents_toolcalling_traces_think, s1k_1.1_think, LongAlign_64k_Qwen3_32B_yarn_131k_think, aya_dataset_Qwen3_32B_think, multilingual8_Qwen3_32B_think, OpenThoughts3_1.2M_think, table_gpt_Qwen3_32B_think). Mixed weights 0.02–1.0; 5 epochs.
Final DPO/APO uses two preference splits of smoltalk2 'Preference' config (llama_3.1_tulu_3_8b_preference_mixture_no_think weight 0.5, tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think weight 0.25).

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: Qwen3-32B in reasoning (thinking) mode generated synthetic reasoning traces used in SFT for domains lacking such data (multi-turn conversations, multilinguality, everyday conversations); these synthetic completions form a portion of the 10 reasoning-mode SFT datasets.

anchor_list.excerpt: we generated synthetic data by prompting Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode with prompts from existing non-reasoning datasets
anchor_list.explanation: Names Qwen3-32B (thinking mode) as the generator of synthetic reasoning training data for SFT.

anchor_list.excerpt: We selected generations from Qwen3-32B as 'chosen' and responses from Qwen3-0.6B as 'rejected' for alignment with Anchored Preference Optimization.
anchor_list.explanation: Names Qwen3-32B as the chosen-completion generator for SmolLM3 APO preference pairs.
anchor_list.position: Post-training > Off-policy model alignment with APO

anchor_list.excerpt: SmolTalk (multilingual-8languages): 244736 rows generated with Qwen3-32B with the prompts in SmolTalk. ... generate responses with Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode.
anchor_list.explanation: Multiple think splits explicitly cite Qwen3-32B as their response generator.

anchor_list.excerpt: Tulu 3 8B Preference Mixture (`think`): 216k rows where we generate the chosen responses with Qwen3-32B
anchor_list.explanation: Names Qwen3-32B as the chosen-completion generator for the think preference split.
anchor_list.position: ### Preference Data section, think variant description

anchor_list.excerpt: we generated synthetic data by prompting Qwen3-32B in reasoning mode with prompts from existing non-reasoning datasets
anchor_list.explanation: Source identifies Qwen3-32B as the generator of synthetic reasoning-mode SFT data.
anchor_list.position: Supervised Finetuning paragraph on synthetic data

anchor_list.excerpt: We selected generations from Qwen3-32B as "chosen" and responses from Qwen3-0.6B as "rejected" for alignment with Anchored Preference Optimization.
anchor_list.explanation: Source identifies Qwen3-32B as the chosen-completion generator for APO.

anchor_list.excerpt: split: smoltalk_everyday_convs_reasoning_Qwen3_32B_think ... split: smoltalk_systemchats_Qwen3_32B_think ... split: LongAlign_64k_Qwen3_32B_yarn_131k_think ... split: aya_dataset_Qwen3_32B_think ... split: smoltalk_multilingual8_Qwen3_32B_think ... split: table_gpt_Qwen3_32B_think
anchor_list.explanation: Six SFT splits explicitly name Qwen3-32B (thinking) as the trace generator.

anchor_list.excerpt: split: tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think
anchor_list.explanation: Split name encodes Qwen3-32B as the larger generator (chosen).

description_variants: Qwen3-32B in reasoning (thinking) mode generated synthetic reasoning traces used in SFT for domains lacking such data (multi-turn conversations, multilinguality, everyday conversations); these synthetic completions form a portion of the 10 reasoning-mode SFT datasets.
Qwen3-32B generated the chosen completions for SmolLM3's reasoning-mode synthetic preference pairs used in APO.

Qwen3-32B generated the response/think traces for many SFT think splits in smoltalk2: smoltalk_multilingual8 (244736), smoltalk_everyday_convs_reasoning (2057), smoltalk_systemchats (27436), aya_dataset (15222), table_gpt (13201), LongAlign-64k-yarn-131k (7526), and Multi-Turn IF responses (28217). It is the dominant `_think` generator in the SFT mix.
Qwen3-32B generated the chosen completions (preferred responses) for the synthetic `tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think` split of smoltalk2 Preference (216385 rows, weight 0.25).

Qwen3-32B (in reasoning mode) was prompted with prompts from existing non-reasoning datasets to generate synthetic reasoning-mode SFT data covering multi-turn conversations, multilinguality, and everyday conversations — gaps the team could not otherwise fill from public reasoning datasets.
Qwen3-32B generations served as the 'chosen' completions in synthetic preference pairs for reasoning-mode APO alignment of SmolLM3-3B; pairs complement the Tulu3 non-reasoning data.

Several SFT splits in smoltalk2 SFT config carry traces generated by Qwen3-32B (thinking mode): smoltalk_everyday_convs_reasoning_Qwen3_32B_think, smoltalk_systemchats_Qwen3_32B_think, LongAlign_64k_Qwen3_32B_yarn_131k_think, aya_dataset_Qwen3_32B_think, smoltalk_multilingual8_Qwen3_32B_think, table_gpt_Qwen3_32B_think.
tulu_3_8b_pref_mix_Qwen3_32B_Qwen3_0.6B_think split bundles preference pairs whose 'chosen' answers were generated by Qwen3-32B (thinking mode); used in SmolLM3-3B's DPO mixture.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: The multi-turn-reasoning-if_think SFT split follows the methodology of Meta's Multi-IF (https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.15553) for synthesizing multi-turn instruction-following data, adopting its schema of 2 follow-up verifiable turns per prompt.

anchor_list.excerpt: We follow [Multi-IF's approach](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.15553) to generate multi turn data.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM3-3B
edges.description: The smoltalk-multilingual-8languages no_think split follows the Qwen 2.5 report's translation recipe: translate Smol-Magpie-Ultra and Smol-Constraints prompts into 8 target languages (fr, es, it, pt, de, ar, ru, zh) respecting local conventions, then generate target-language answers.

anchor_list.excerpt: Following [Qwen 2.5 report](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2412.15115), we first translate the prompts in Smol-Magpie-Ultra and Smol-Constraints using Qwen to 8 languages (fr, es, it, pt, de, ar, ru, zh) while respecting local conventions
anchor_list.explanation: Explicit methodology borrowing from the Qwen 2.5 multilingual translation pipeline.
anchor_list.position: ### SFT no_think list, smoltalk multilingual entry
